breaks: LN item iv.'s literal one-sided filter `L_{do(W)} := L ∖ { (v_1,v_2) ∈ L | v_2 ∈ W }`; mirror pairs `(w, v)` with `w ∈ W` and `v ∉ W` are kept by the literal LN reading but dropped by the Lean's two-sided filter `e.1 ∉ W ∧ e.2 ∉ W`.
preserves: The LN's natural-language gloss "remove all edges into nodes from W" read symmetrically (= remove every bidirected edge with at least one endpoint in W); the closure-under-symmetry axiom `hL_symm` on the resulting CDMG; the intended symmetric semantics of the `\huh` notation declared in `def-not-cdmg`.
at_risk_pattern: hardInterventionOn.*\.L membership reads that assume the LN-literal one-sided rule "only the second component matters"; case-splits on which endpoint of a bidirected edge lies in W under an intervention; any new CDMG-constructing operator that filters L by an endpoint predicate. Grep targets: `hardInterventionOn`, `\\huh.*\\doit`, `L_\{\\doit(`, `bidirected.*intervention`.
notes: Forced by `def_3_1`'s storage convention — L is a `Finset (Node × Node)` with a separate `hL_symm : ∀ {v1 v2}, (v1, v2) ∈ L → (v2, v1) ∈ L` axiom. The literal LN formula filters by the second component only; applied to a symmetric L this leaves a non-symmetric residue (mirror pair `(w, v)` survives while `(v, w)` is removed), which cannot be packaged as a CDMG.L field without violating `hL_symm`. The verifier's `verify_with_examples` worker confirmed the divergence on a concrete two-node instance (G2 over Fin 2, L = {(0,1),(1,0)}, W = {1}: LN-literal gives {(1,0)}, Lean gives ∅). The symmetric reading is what the LN's own natural-language gloss endorses, and is the only reading consistent with `def_3_1`'s `hL_symm`. The rewritten canonical tex (`def_3_10_HardInterventionOn.tex`) makes the deviation explicit in its "Remark on item iv. and the symmetry of L" paragraph. Downstream consumers (do-calculus, ch. 5+; iSCM intervention algebra, ch. 8+; `claim_3_18` marginalisation-intervention interaction) inherit the symmetric semantics, which is what mathematical practice in the LN actually uses. No purely-bidirected lemma should depend on the literal one-sided form; if one does, that is the place to revisit. The deviation is structural (forced by upstream encoding choice in `def_3_1`), not a local choice that could be undone without a chapter-wide refactor of L's storage representation.

breaks: At a walk position k adjacent to a directed self-loop edge (v,v) in G.E, the LN's literal stored-pair "edge into v_k" test (def-edge-relations item ii) would contribute one arrowhead at v_k from each adjacent walk step; the side-aware Lean encoding (refactor_HeadAtTarget / refactor_HeadAtSource consumed by refactor_IsCollider) contributes 1 only on the walk-traversal target end of each step and 0 on the source end.
preserves: On walks with no directed self-loop, the side-aware and literal stored-pair readings coincide exactly; the collider / non-collider partition of every walk position is total and now also mutually exclusive (the LN's implicit invariant, silently relied on but technically violated by the literal wording at directed self-loops where both the left-chain non-collider pattern and the collider pattern fire simultaneously).
at_risk_pattern: A walk step is .forwardE or .backwardE on a self-loop edge (u,u) in G.E and a downstream proof reasons about IsCollider / IsNonCollider at the adjacent position via the literal pattern-macro / stored-pair reading.
notes:
This row (def_3_15, CollidersAndNon) is the root of the collider_side_aware refactor. The literal LN block at def_3_15 reads each position's classification via four non-collider patterns or one collider pattern; the macros decode to "\tuh between u,v iff (u,v) in E" and "\hut between u,v iff (v,u) in E". At a directed self-loop (v,v) in E both macros fire simultaneously, so a position adjacent to such a loop literally matches BOTH the left-chain non-collider pattern AND the collider pattern. The LN wording-check report for this row explicitly flagged this contradiction (subtlety id "self_loop_makes_tuh_and_hut_simultaneously_true"). The "i.e. at most one / two arrowheads" glosses are likewise ambiguous: the LN never specifies whether a self-loop contributes 0, 1, or 2 arrowheads at the (single) loop node.

The refactor resolves the ambiguity decisively via a side-aware reading. Each walk step's typed WalkStep G u v constructor (.forwardE: head at type-target v; .backwardE: head at type-source u; .bidir: heads at both) carries the walk-traversal direction explicitly, even on syntactically symmetric self-loops where the two distinct WalkStep G v v constructors coexist on the same stored pair. The contribution to the arrowhead count at v_k reads off the constructor tag alone (no node-equality test against v_k), so the u=v=w collapse that produced the over-classification of the predecessor IsCollider on walks of shape a -> b -> b is structurally impossible. Concretely: refactor_HeadAtTarget is True on .forwardE / .bidir / False on .backwardE; refactor_HeadAtSource is True on .backwardE / .bidir / False on .forwardE; refactor_IsCollider at an internal position pairs the head-at-target side of the incoming step with the head-at-source side of the outgoing step.

This entry serves in lieu of a populated addition_to_the_LN clause (tag [collider_side_aware_walkstep_predicates]). The addition write was attempted on turns 12, 15, 17, 19 of this row; on each subsequent turn the field read length 0 from disk via fresh Python subprocess. The git diff for refactor_data.json confirms the orchestrator rewrites the file across turn boundaries (preserving only actions_tracking / agent_registry / time_needed_to_solve updates), so manager-direct edits to addition_to_the_LN do not survive. The semantic content of the planned addition (three labelled clauses (a)/(b)/(c) plus closing canonical-classification clause) is encoded into the design-choice comment blocks above refactor_HeadAtTarget / refactor_HeadAtSource / refactor_IsCollider in CollidersAndNon.lean, and is restated in compressed form in the breaks / preserves / at_risk_pattern fields above.

Downstream consumers in the collider_side_aware refactor table (claim_3_22 SigmaSeparationSymmetric, claim_3_27 LabelRoman and any later walk-based reasoning that case-analyzes collider / non-collider status at a self-loop-adjacent position) should grep against the at_risk_pattern and use the side-aware predicates rather than the literal pattern-macro reading. On walks without directed self-loops (the overwhelming majority of CDMG examples in the lecture notes), the side-aware and literal readings coincide exactly, so existing intuition and proofs port verbatim.

breaks: The literal stored-pair test of def_3_2 item ii fires the E-disjunct or L-disjunct at both adjacent positions of a walk-step a_i regardless of which channel the walk actually recorded as traversed. The side-aware reading commits to the recorded WalkStep constructor (.forwardE / .backwardE / .bidir) as the sole signal for the per-step arrowhead contribution at adjacent positions k ∈ {i, i+1}. The two readings disagree on two distinct corner kinds: (i) directed self-loop walk-steps a_i ∈ E with v_i = v_{i+1} (no coexisting L-edge), where the literal test contributes 1+1=2 unconditionally at the shared adjacent position while the side-aware reading commits via the recorded constructor to a single position (the walk-traversal target) for the contribution, yielding 1+0=1; (ii) writing-mirror walk-steps where (v_i, v_{i+1}) ∈ E AND s(v_i, v_{i+1}) ∈ L with v_i ≠ v_{i+1}, traversed via the E-channel (constructor .forwardE or .backwardE), where the literal L-disjunct fires at BOTH adjacent positions regardless of the recorded channel while the side-aware reading ignores the coexisting L-edge entirely and contributes only at the walk-traversal target of the recorded directed channel.
preserves: On walks that traverse NEITHER a directed self-loop step NOR a writing-mirror walk-step via the E-channel (i.e., walks avoiding both corner kinds (i) and (ii) above), the side-aware HeadAtTarget / HeadAtSource predicates coincide pointwise with the literal stored-pair "edge into v_k" test of def_3_2 item ii at every adjacent position. The aggregate arrowhead count ah_π(k) and its threshold-based collider / non-collider classification (non-collider iff ah_π(k) ≤ 1; collider iff ah_π(k) = 2) agree exactly on such walks. Writing-mirror walk-steps traversed via the .bidir (L-channel) constructor also agree pointwise with the literal test, since both readings fire the L-disjunct at both adjacent positions in that case. Decidability of the side-aware predicates and the addition's canonical classification clause are preserved.
at_risk_pattern: any downstream proof that destructs WalkStep cases and assumes the literal stored-pair test for arrowhead contributions at adjacent positions; any proof citing def_3_2 item ii's "edge into v_k" predicate directly on a walk-step without case-splitting on the .forwardE / .backwardE / .bidir constructor tag; any d/σ-separation, σ-blocking, or back-door / front-door argument over walks that may include a directed self-loop step OR a writing-mirror E-traversal step; greps for "L.contains", "s(v, w) ∈ G.L", "Sym2 ⟨v_i, v_{i+1}⟩", or "(v_i, v_{i+1}) ∈ G.E" appearing near collider / non-collider classifications without a constructor case-split; greps for "HeadAtTarget" / "HeadAtSource" / "ah_π" / "arrowhead_count" / "IsCollider" / "IsNonCollider" used alongside def_3_2 stored-pair phrasing; any consumer of CollidersAndNon that argues "L-edge between adjacent walk vertices contributes arrowheads at both endpoints" without restricting to the .bidir constructor.
notes: This entry supersedes the narrower `collider_side_aware_at_self_loops` entry in scope. The widening is mandated by the strict-equivalence checker's verdict on def_3_15: a concrete CDMG with E={(v₀, v₁)}, L={s(v₀, v₁)}, v₀ ≠ v₁ (writing-mirror, permitted by def_3_1's hL_irrefl since s(v₀, v₁) is non-diagonal), walk cons v₀ (.forwardE h_E) (.nil v₁ _) — at position 0 the literal stored-pair test gives ah_π(0) = 1 (L-disjunct fires unconditionally) while the side-aware encoding gives ah_π(0) = 0 (refactor_HeadAtSource on .forwardE _ matches the False branch with no consultation of G.L). No directed self-loop is involved in this witness. The original narrow entry's `preserves` clause asserted "on walks with no directed self-loop, the two readings coincide" — that claim is FALSE on writing-mirror walks, so this widened entry is the authoritative record. The Lean encoding (refactor_HeadAtTarget / refactor_HeadAtSource / refactor_IsCollider / refactor_IsNonCollider) already correctly implements both corner-kind deviations and needs no edits; the canonical statement tex (def_3_15_CollidersAndNon.tex, addition clause (c)) already correctly describes both corner kinds. The discrepancy was purely between the deviation register's narrow scope text and the wider scope actually implemented and documented. Downstream consumers of CollidersAndNon throughout chapter 3 (σ-blocking, σ-separation, d-separation, the claim_3_25 cluster) MUST treat arrowhead counts in this codebase as CHANNEL-AWARE (constructor-driven), NOT stored-pair-aware (graph-data-driven). See addition_to_the_LN clause (c) of def_3_15 for the authoritative disambiguation, and the design-block comments above refactor_HeadAtTarget / refactor_HeadAtSource in CollidersAndNon.lean for the rationale and the strict-deviation scope.
